# UC-19.1.39 · Proxmox Ceph Cluster HEALTH_WARN and OSD Flap Detection

## Description

Hyperconverged Proxmox clusters using Ceph depend on quorum, MON, and OSD stability. HEALTH_WARN often precedes data unavailability when additional failures stack. Early detection buys time to replace OSDs or fix networks before pools go read-only.

## Value

Prevents surprise VM I/O stalls by alerting on Ceph health regression while the cluster can still self-heal.

## Implementation

Poll `ceph -s -f json` every 60–120s from a privileged service account and index as `proxmox:ceph`, or forward ceph daemon syslog. Extract `health.status`, `osdmap` counts, and slow/unfound PG hints if present. Page on first non-OK health. Trend OSD `up/in` counts for flap detection.

## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization (sourcetype="proxmox:ceph" OR (sourcetype=syslog program=ceph)) earliest=-1h
| eval hs=coalesce(health_status, health)
| where hs!="HEALTH_OK" OR like(_raw, "%down%") OR like(_raw, "%flapp%")
| stats latest(hs) as health, values(message) as detail by host
| sort health
```

## Visualization

Single value (cluster health), Timechart (OSD up count), Table (hosts reporting warnings).

## References

- [Ceph Documentation — Monitoring OSDs](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/rados/operations/monitoring-osd-pg/)
- [Proxmox VE Wiki — Ceph Monitor](https://pve.proxmox.com/wiki/Deploy_Hyper-Converged_Ceph_Cluster)
